plan_adherence_notes: The branch aligns with the planning artifacts only on the audit premise. `research.json` concludes no preserved sentinel was weakened, and `plan.json` says the default outcome is no restorative test edit and no backend change unless a preserved sentinel fails. Commit `4d650c77` still modifies `backend/server.py` even though the preserved auth/rate-limit sentinel slice in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` currently passes cleanly (`pytest -q tests/test_bayes_market_api.py -k BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` => `21 passed`). Comment-post and event-trade also remain success-path-only, so the main scope breach is the unjustified production-code refactor.
missed_questions: Where is the task-local `questions.json`? Its absence leaves no auditable record of the exact questions that were supposed to drive this step.
Did any preserved sentinel actually fail or widen in this checkout before permitting a `backend/server.py` change?
If the audit stayed clean, should the branch remain a no-op outside `.fleet/tasks/` for this phase?
reason: The local research and plan both say this phase should only restore affected tests, and should touch `backend/server.py` only if a preserved sentinel exposes a real shared-seam defect. No such failing sentinel is present: the focused auth/rate-limit sentinel class passes cleanly (`21 passed`), yet commit `4d650c77` changes production code. That violates the current phase scope and the instruction to avoid production-code changes here.
